mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate-cut periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals impending economic contraction, causing investors to price in lower future earnings and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the UNRATE threshold breach, or the signal exhibits no predictive power in post-2008 data.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts and industrial production confirms broad-based demand expansion across residential and manufacturing sectors, driving higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative or zero information coefficient over a 3-year out-of-sample test, or forward returns do not differ significantly from the median when either series diverges.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle inflationary environment, accelerating CPIAUCSL combined with lagging FEDFUNDS compresses real yields and triggers equity risk premium expansion, depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL accelerates above 3.0% annualized while FEDFUNDS stays below 1.0%, or the regime-specific alpha decays to zero after controlling for VIX levels.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales and financial service revenues indicate immediate consumer spending strength, which directly supports near-term corporate earnings guidance and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with RSAFS momentum, or the signal reverses direction during high-volatility market regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production dropping below the 95.0 index level signals a supply-side contraction and weakening capacity utilization, leading to margin compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly negative when INDPRO breaches the 95.0 threshold, or the predictive relationship vanishes when adjusted for global manufacturing PMI data.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a healthy labor market expansion, reducing recession probabilities and supporting equity valuations for positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in a multivariate regression, or forward returns fail to outperform when PAYEMS and UNRATE move in opposite directions.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, accelerating real GDP growth coupled with rising housing starts indicates structural demand normalization, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain flat or negative when GDPC1 accelerates above 2.5% and HOUST exceeds 1.0 million, or the mechanism fails to predict returns during stagflationary periods.
